description: Having the qualities of forest - sheltering, ancient, interconnected, breathing. It describes places thick with trees, rich with undergrowth, alive with the wisdom of root and branch.
rationale: The word 'shali' uses the whispering fricative 'sh' to evoke the rustle of leaves and forest sounds. The flowing 'li' suggests the continuous life force of the woods. This simple two-syllable structure captures both the shelter ('sha') and vitality ('li') of forested spaces.
